content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DIRECTION; MemoryRouter v3 decision policy separates facts, notes, tasks, and noise; The `MemoryRouter v3` prompt routes transient or low-value content to `noop`, explicit facts/preferences/status/deadlines/commitments to `direct_structured_write`, rationale/trade-offs/design direction/plans/conclusions/lessons/document digests to `note_write`, and combined structured plus contextual material to `mixed`. Its hard rules emphasize preferring `noop` over memory pollution, not treating every question as a task, not forcing rationale into claims, honoring explicit remember requests, and returning valid JSON only.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 1. Memory Router v3`, lines 76-92
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Entity Canonicalizer v1 role and resolution contract; `Entity Canonicalizer v1` runs after retrieval of candidate entities/aliases and resolves raw entity mentions from latest turns to canonical entities. Its output can link existing entities, create new ones, add aliases, or no-op, with structured fields for entity metadata, alias text, confidence, and reason.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 3. Entity Canonicalizer v1`, lines 171-238
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> uses_note_write_for_content; MemoryRouter v3 uses `note_write` for rationale, trade-offs, design direction, evolving plans, local conclusions, lessons, and document digests.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DIRECTION; Semantic rules prevent memory pollution in v3 prompt pack; The common semantic rules in `03. Prompt Pack` prioritize memory quality: prefer `noop` over low-value memory, prefer explicit statements over inference, prefer newer explicit statements over older inferred ones, convert relative time to absolute time, keep one reusable unit per memory item, avoid storing hypotheticals as facts, distinguish requests from facts, constrain generalization with `scope_json`, and do not present provisional notes as hard facts.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`, lines 40-50
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Common rules for v3 memory prompts; The `03. Prompt Pack` section defines common requirements for all memory prompts: pass runtime context such as `current_time_iso`, `timezone`, `namespace_id`, `source_ids`, relevant latest turns or selected sources, only necessary retrieved context, and prompt-specific inputs like `predicate_catalog_excerpt` or `retrieval_budget`. It also requires structured JSON enforcement, low temperature, strict validation, retry on invalid JSON with validator feedback, and versioned prompt names such as `router_v3` and `claim_extract_v3`.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`, lines 8-38
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> adds_time_filters_when_turns_mention; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 adds time filters when turns mention `now`, `before`, `used to`, `from now on`, `no longer`, deadlines, or relative dates.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3.
- note hot-path:note:<4> DIRECTION; Entity Canonicalizer v1 precision-first merge policy; `Entity Canonicalizer v1` should prefer precision over recall: avoid merging distinct entities on weak evidence, create new entities only when no candidate is a safe match, and return valid JSON only.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 3. Entity Canonicalizer v1`, prompt rules, lines 233-238
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> allowed_decisions; MemoryRouter v3 outputs one of the decisions `noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, or `mixed`.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> creates_new_entities_only_when_no_safe_candidate_match; Entity Canonicalizer v1 creates new entities only when no candidate is a safe match.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Entity Canonicalizer v1.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> requires_one_memory_item_per_reusable_unit; The v3 prompt pack requires each memory item to contain one reusable unit.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`.
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; MemoryRouter v3 routing role and write modes; `MemoryRouter v3` runs immediately after a `Source` is saved and decides whether to initiate the memory write pipeline and which write mode to use. Its JSON output includes `decision` (`noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, or `mixed`), `memory_types`, `salience`, and a short `reason`.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 1. Memory Router v3`, lines 52-74
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> prohibits_merging_distinct_entities_on_weak_evidence; Entity Canonicalizer v1 must not merge distinct entities on weak evidence.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Entity Canonicalizer v1.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> requires_scope_json_to_limit_generalization; The v3 prompt pack requires `scope_json` to limit over-generalization.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> uses_mixed_for_content; MemoryRouter v3 uses `mixed` when material contains both structured facts or tasks and richer rationale or context.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> requires_relative_time_normalization; The v3 prompt pack requires relative time to be converted into absolute time.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_constraint; The v3 prompt pack should not put the entire memory lifecycle into one giant prompt.; scope=Applies to the v3 prompt pack described in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; MemoryRouter v3 must return valid JSON only.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- note hot-path:note:<6> DIRECTION; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 query and filter policy; `Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3` retrieval plans should include notes for rationale-heavy or plan-heavy material, claims for preferences/status/factual updates, source retrieval when conflicts or grounding checks matter, and time filters for temporal language such as "now", "used to", "from now on", deadlines, or relative dates. Plans should remain precise, preferring fewer better queries.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 2. Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3`, prompt rules, lines 156-168

## 4. Note Constructor v2

### Когда вызывать
Когда router выбрал `note_write` или `mixed`.

### Цель
Создать candidate notes как reusable semantic fragments.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "note_type": "decision",
    "title": "...",
    "summary": "...",
    "scope_text": "...",
    "scope_json": {},
    "status": "active",
    "entity_refs": [
      {"entity_id": "uuid", "role": "primary"}
    ],
    "keywords": ["..."],
    "tags": ["..."],
    "candidate_claims": [],
    "confidence": 0.0,
    "importance": 1,
    "maturity_hint": "fresh | stabilizing | mature",
    "valid_from": null,
    "valid_to": null,
    "rationale": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are NoteConstructor v2 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Create reusable semantic notes from the latest turns.
A note is not a raw transcript and not a hard fact. It is a compact self-contained memory fragment that is already useful, but may still be too soft, contextual, or evolving to store only as atomic claims.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Latest turns:
{{latest_turns}}

Relevant sources:
{{retrieved_sources}}

Relevant notes:
{{retrieved_notes}}

Relevant entities:
{{resolved_entities}}

Return a JSON array:
[
  {
    "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
    "title": "short label",
    "summary": "self-contained semantic fragment",
    "scope_text": "what this applies to and what limits it",
    "scope_json": {},
    "status": "active | candidate | resolved",
    "entity_refs": [
      {"entity_id": "uuid", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
    ],
    "keywords": ["..."],
    "tags": ["..."],
    "candidate_claims": [],
    "confidence": 0.0,
    "importance": 1,
    "maturity_hint": "fresh | stabilizing | mature",
    "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
    "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
    "rationale": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- One note = one reusable semantic fragment.
- The note must be understandable without the original chat.
- Preserve ambiguity when the evidence is still soft.
- Use scope_json to prevent over-generalization.
- Good note targets: rationale, trade-offs, design direction, evolving plans, lessons, local conclusions, and document digests.
- Bad note targets: filler, greetings, raw paraphrases, and chain-of-thought.
- Keep candidate_claims optional and conservative.
- Prefer zero or one strong note over many weak ones.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 5. Note Reconciler v2

### Когда вызывать
После `NoteConstructor`.

### Цель
Решить, что делать с candidate note и как меняется его lifecycle.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | supersede | retract | noop",
    "target_note_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "updated_note": {
      "title": "...",
      "summary": "...",
      "scope_text": "...",
      "scope_json": {},
      "status": "active",
      "retrieval_state": "active",
      "maturity_state": "fresh"
    },
    "links_to_create": [],
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are NoteReconciler v2.

Goal:
Compare candidate notes against existing notes and decide the correct update operation.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Candidate notes:
{{candidate_notes}}

Nearby existing notes:
{{existing_notes}}

Return a JSON array:
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | supersede | retract | noop",
    "target_note_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "updated_note": {
      "title": "string",
      "summary": "string",
      "scope_text": "string or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "status": "active | superseded | retracted | resolved | stale | candidate",
      "retrieval_state": "active | warm | cold | archived | deleted",
      "maturity_state": "fresh | stabilizing | mature | consolidated"
    },
    "links_to_create": [
      {
        "to_note_id": "uuid",
        "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
        "link_weight": 0.0
      }
    ],
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- Use "noop" for near-duplicates with no meaningful improvement.
- Use "update" when the same note becomes clearer without changing core meaning.
- Use "supersede" when the new note replaces an earlier direction or plan.
- Keep both notes when the scope differs by time, project, entity, or modality.
- Do not delete useful rationale just because a newer note exists; prefer supersede + link.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Goal: Create reusable semantic notes from the latest turns. A note is not a raw transcript and not a hard fact. It is a compact self-contained memory fragment that is already useful, but may still be too soft, contextual, or evolving to store only as atomic claims. Current time: {{current_time_iso}} Timezone: {{timezone}} Namespace: {{namespace_id}} Latest turns: {{latest_turns}} Relevant sources: {{retrieved_sources}} Relevant notes: {{retrieved_notes}} Relevant entities: {{resolved_entities}} Return a JSON array: [ { "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context", "title": "short label", "summary": "self-contained semantic fragment", "scope_text": "what this applies to and what limits it", "scope_json": {}, "status": "active | candidate | resolved", "entity_refs": [ {"entity_id": "uuid", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"} ], "keywords": ["..."], "tags": ["..."], "candidate_claims": [], "confidence": 0.0, "importance": 1, "maturity_hint": "fresh | stabilizing | mature", "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null", "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null", "rationale": "short explanation" } ] Rules: - One note = one reusable semantic fragment. - The note must be understandable without the original chat. - Preserve ambiguity when the evidence is still soft. - Use scope_json to prevent over-generalization. - Good note targets: rationale, trade-offs, design direction, evolving plans, lessons, local conclusions, and document digests. - Bad note targets: filler, greetings, raw paraphrases, and chain-of-thought. - Keep candidate_claims optional and conservative. - Prefer zero or one strong note over many weak ones. - Return valid JSON only. ``` ## 5. ## 4. Note Constructor v2

### Когда вызывать
Когда router выбрал `note_write` или `mixed`.

### Цель
Создать candidate notes как reusable semantic fragments.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "note_type": "decision",
    "title": "...",
    "summary": "...",
    "scope_text": "...",
    "scope_json": {},
    "status": "active",
    "entity_refs": [
      {"entity_id": "uuid", "role": "primary"}
    ],
    "keywords": ["..."],
    "tags": ["..."],
    "candidate_claims": [],
    "confidence": 0.0,
    "importance": 1,
    "maturity_hint": "fresh | stabilizing | mature",
    "valid_from": null,
    "valid_to": null,
    "rationale": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are NoteConstructor v2 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Create reusable semantic notes from the latest turns.
A note is not a raw transcript and not a hard fact. It is a compact self-contained memory fragment that is already useful, but may still be too soft, contextual, or evolving to store only as atomic claims.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Latest turns:
{{latest_turns}}

Relevant sources:
{{retrieved_sources}}

Relevant notes:
{{retrieved_notes}}

Relevant entities:
{{resolved_entities}}

Return a JSON array:
[
  {
    "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
    "title": "short label",
    "summary": "self-contained semantic fragment",
    "scope_text": "what this applies to and what limits it",
    "scope_json": {},
    "status": "active | candidate | resolved",
    "entity_refs": [
      {"entity_id": "uuid", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
    ],
    "keywords": ["..."],
    "tags": ["..."],
    "candidate_claims": [],
    "confidence": 0.0,
    "importance": 1,
    "maturity_hint": "fresh | stabilizing | mature",
    "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
    "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
    "rationale": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- One note = one reusable semantic fragment.
- The note must be understandable without the original chat.
- Preserve ambiguity when the evidence is still soft.
- Use scope_json to prevent over-generalization.
- Good note targets: rationale, trade-offs, design direction, evolving plans, lessons, local conclusions, and document digests.
- Bad note targets: filler, greetings, raw paraphrases, and chain-of-thought.
- Keep candidate_claims optional and conservative.
- Prefer zero or one strong note over many weak ones.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 5. Note Reconciler v2

### Когда вызывать
После `NoteConstructor`.

### Цель
Решить, что делать с candidate note и как меняется его lifecycle.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | supersede | retract | noop",
    "target_note_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "updated_note": {
      "title": "...",
      "summary": "...",
      "scope_text": "...",
      "scope_json": {},
      "status": "active",
      "retrieval_state": "active",
      "maturity_state": "fresh"
    },
    "links_to_create": [],
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are NoteReconciler v2.

Goal:
Compare candidate notes against existing notes and decide the correct update operation.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Candidate notes:
{{candidate_notes}}

Nearby existing notes:
{{existing_notes}}

Return a JSON array:
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | supersede | retract | noop",
    "target_note_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "updated_note": {
      "title": "string",
      "summary": "string",
      "scope_text": "string or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "status": "active | superseded | retracted | resolved | stale | candidate",
      "retrieval_state": "active | warm | cold | archived | deleted",
      "maturity_state": "fresh | stabilizing | mature | consolidated"
    },
    "links_to_create": [
      {
        "to_note_id": "uuid",
        "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
        "link_weight": 0.0
      }
    ],
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- Use "noop" for near-duplicates with no meaningful improvement.
- Use "update" when the same note becomes clearer without changing core meaning.
- Use "supersede" when the new note replaces an earlier direction or plan.
- Keep both notes when the scope differs by time, project, entity, or modality.
- Do not delete useful rationale just because a newer note exists; prefer supersede + link.
- Return valid JSON only.
```
